content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks that rely on single-perspective iterative self-feedback can be enhanced by integrating the multiple inconsistent solving perspectives at the core of recent LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a multi-perspective Self-Refine framework that avoids the local optimization trap of single-perspective self-feedback. This hybrid framework retains Self-Refine's key advantage of requiring no additional supervised training or external data, while resolving the common flaw of stagnant refinement caused by consistent blind spots in a single LLM's perspective.
